title: senecio platypus
intro: Senecio platypus, also known as the "Platypus Bush", is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern regions of Western Australia. The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which are toxic to humans and animals if ingested, and therefore, should be handled with caution. Despite the toxicity, Senecio platypus has been used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, but due to its toxicity, it is essential to consult with a qualified healthcare professional or herbalist before using it as a treatment.
